title: The Prince
timelinePosition: Directly follows Chapter 14 on the road south. Opens on the approach to Hearthglen and closes with Jaina, Sylvanas, and Cyndia riding ahead of Arthas's main column toward Stratholme to try to kill the plague at its source before the city fully turns.
openLocation: South road into Hearthglen's blighted outskirts.
transport: Horseback approach from the Eastweald; on foot through Hearthglen and Arthas's camp; back to horseback for the ride toward Stratholme.
immediateObjective: Reach Arthas, hand over the full plague picture, and decide whether there is still any move left before Stratholme goes under.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Sylvanas's perception only.
No interior access to Arthas, Jaina, or Uther.
No on-page Culling yet; Stratholme remains future pressure rather than present action.
No clean repair of the Scholomance trust scar; the chapter can only re-align the partnership through work.

povKnowsAtOpen: Jaina gave her the Lich King chain, the Burning Legion threat behind it, the resonance mechanism, the ward-failure problem, and Kel'Thuzad's expected lich return.
Jaina still withheld the room, the method, and the inner turn inside Scholomance, so the trust scar is active.
Sylvanas pointed Arthas at this crisis in Chapter 11 because she needed a prince and an army aimed at the plague.
Hearthglen has already been hit, Uther is on the field, and Arthas is moving fast enough that the road south may already be too late.
The repeated farewell has already surfaced three times across the investigation.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: Whether Arthas can still hear intelligence without turning it straight into speed.
The full history between Arthas and Jaina.
Whether Jaina's new plague theory can actually be used in the field or only explained after the fact.
What Uther has already concluded and how long he has known it.
What the farewell pattern means if it surfaces again.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No interior access to Arthas, Jaina, or Uther.
No clean repair of the partnership crack.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Hearthglen shows the plague has moved into full-force attack, with the dead still rising and being driven forward even after Kel'Thuzad's death.
Arthas has the full plague picture and is marching his main force on Stratholme at once.
Jaina has a narrow source plan: get ahead of the army, hit the granaries and bakeries, and try to stop the city from turning before it finishes seeding itself.
Sylvanas rides with that plan, which gives her the first external proof that Jaina means to spend the Scholomance knowledge to save people, not sit with it.
Uther is still with the column, but fear is already visible in him.
The farewell pattern reaches a fourth sighting in the camp's departure rush.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: Accurate intelligence should let her steer the prince toward useful force.
pov.surfaceObjective: Hand Arthas everything he needs and keep ahead of the plague's pace.
pov.pressureSource: Hearthglen's ruin, Uther's unease, the partnership scar beside her, and a prince with an army she does not command.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Sylvanas still wants to treat Arthas like a lever she can aim instead of a man whose speed has already outrun caution.
pov.chapterTest: The chapter asks whether intelligence creates control here or only creates a faster disaster.
pov.forcedChoice: Trust Arthas's main march alone, or ride behind Jaina's smaller, riskier source plan.
pov.endStateShift: Sylvanas still reads Arthas as a fuse, but Jaina stops reading as a passive source and starts reading as a woman trying to use the theory before it is too late.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 16 opens on the head-start ride and the source attempt under a shrinking city clock.

throughPov.visibleFunction: Prince, commander, and hard center of the camp's pace.
throughPov.povMisreadAtOpen: Sylvanas wants to read him as ally first and danger second.
throughPov.correctionEarnedHere: Everything about Hearthglen and the war camp says he converts bad news straight into motion.
throughPov.interactionRule: No interiority. Use armor damage, titles, hands on maps, interruptions, and what he does when given time-sensitive options.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Damaged pair inside someone else's war machine, with Cyndia on the left.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: Jaina does more than brief. She immediately tries to turn Kel'Thuzad's teaching into a rescue plan, and Sylvanas has to reckon with that in real time.
team.namingAttitudeRule: Keep the public competence clean and the private scar in the margins.
team.doNotOverstateRule: This is not repair by speech. It is repair by who rides where when the clock starts.

entries.rule: Partner and source. Do not access her interior. Show her through what she asks for, what she risks, and how fast she turns theory into an attempted field fix.
Holds the left, manages route and relay, and is the first one to hear the fourth farewell clearly enough to matter.
Formal, fast, visibly unrepaired, and already leaning past hesitation.
Healer, mentor, watcher. The Light must read as fully good before Stratholme breaks that trust.
Competent captain and camp gatekeeper. He reads pressure early.
Ordinary face for the fourth farewell sighting. Do not unmask.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 16 opens with Jaina, Sylvanas, and Cyndia riding ahead of the army to try the source-neutralization plan inside Stratholme's shrinking window.

factsLockedForChapter16: Hearthglen proves the plague has moved into full-force attack, with the dead still joining the fight as the field struggles to keep up.
Arthas has the full plague picture and is marching the main army on Stratholme at once.
Jaina has a narrow source plan aimed at granaries, bakeries, and other feed points before the city fully turns.
Sylvanas rides with Jaina's source plan instead of staying with the main column.
Uther remains with the army at chapter close but is already afraid of where the prince is heading.
The farewell has reached a fourth sighting in the camp departure rush.
factsNotYetEarned: Any clean repair conversation between Sylvanas and Jaina.
Any proof yet that Jaina's source plan will work at city scale.

scenes.title: The Held City
scenes.sceneFunction: Show that Hearthglen survived only by brute force against a spreading, self-feeding outbreak, then let Uther confirm that the dead are now coming in numbers that no single kill can end.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Arthas is ahead, Hearthglen has already taken the blow, and the partnership crack is still riding with them.
scenes.arcPressure: Sylvanas put a prince on this road. Hearthglen shows what that road now looks like under full load.

enrichment.detail: Hearthglen should read as a real fortified Lordaeron town rather than a roadside village: walls, towers, chapel, stables, smithy, and a garrison footprint still visible under the siege damage. Canon Hearthglen survives only because Arthas and then Uther keep reclearing the newly dead fast enough to stop a total collapse, which fits the chapter's field-healer and pyre pressure.

beats.goal: Reach Hearthglen and read what the plague has done to the country around it.
beats.conflict: The last miles south are already choking northbound. Refugees jam the firmer side of the road, orchards are black at the tips, wagons drag under too much family weight, and the familiar Eastweald work-road has lost the right to pretend this is still only distance. The three-rider crack is still in the saddle with them.

outcome.summary: They make the miles they need, but every mile turns the plague from mapped intelligence into bodies, exhaustion, and land already lost.

beats.valueShift: southbound urgency -> human cost on the road
newOnPageInformation: The plague had time to settle here in a way the earlier chapters only warned about.
Refugee movement is slow, exhausted, and too large for any local response to absorb cleanly.
Jaina is already straining under the ambient necromantic pressure before Hearthglen comes into view.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What shape Arthas is in after fighting through Hearthglen.
Whether the city is merely scarred or still actively losing ground.
sensoryAnchors: wagon wheels under too much weight
beats.transitionIntent: arrival-pressure: the blighted outskirts give way to a town that is still fighting to stay alive.
beats.verifierNotes: Eastweald is already established occupied farm country. Treat this as a harsher continuation of the same road, not a fresh location introduction.
beats.verifierModification: Tightened the opening road beat so Hearthglen arrival pressure lands faster and chapter14's road texture is not reintroduced as fresh setup.

beats.goal: Assess Hearthglen's state and determine what kind of battle was actually fought here.
beats.conflict: Hearthglen held, but the city is not quiet after the win. Hospital tents are full, pyres are hot, and fresh cries keep rising whenever another body on another stretcher starts moving before the priests can get to it. The dead are not arriving in neat waves. The outbreak is feeding itself.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas confirms the defense was competent, but the field picture says the plague is now attacking in full force and picking up speed.

beats.valueShift: human cost on the road -> outbreak now running hot
newOnPageInformation: Hearthglen's grain had already activated inside a living town before anyone understood the full mechanism.
Newly dead townsfolk and soldiers are still rejoining the attack if the field misses them for even minutes.
The self-mustering choir effect is now strong enough that the dead keep coming without Kel'Thuzad standing over them personally.
The Cult's orders are still being carried out in the field despite Scholomance's loss.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How much of this is blind choir behavior and how much is guided by living handlers.
Whether Stratholme is already farther gone than Hearthglen was.
sensoryAnchors: bells restarting before the last smoke clears
beats.transitionIntent: healer-read: the first clean reading has to come from the man trying to keep the wounded from joining the next wave.
beats.verifierNotes: Hearthglen was already established as a provisional hold. Keep the town's continuing failure as escalation, not revelation.

beats.goal: Get Uther's reading of Hearthglen and Arthas before entering the war camp.
beats.conflict: Uther is healing, not commanding, when Sylvanas finds him. The Light in his hands is warm, ordinary, and openly good. The words he chooses around Arthas are not. Hearthglen held because the defenders kept killing the newly dead faster than the choir could fold them back in.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gets the firsthand read she needs, but Uther's visible goodness only sharpens how bad the prince's road may already be.

beats.valueShift: outbreak now running hot -> warning in human form
newOnPageInformation: Uther is still a field healer first, not only a title.
The city was saved by speed, holy fire, and constant reclearing, not by breaking the plague cycle.
Uther notices the three-rider formation and its scar before formal introductions do any work.
He already fears what Arthas will do in a city larger than this one.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Uther thinks the prince can still be checked.
Whether Arthas will hear anything except urgency once the next city is named.
sensoryAnchors: older plain warhammer at Uther's side
beats.transitionIntent: tent-entry: with Hearthglen priced, the chapter has to enter the command tent where price turns into orders.
beats.verifierNotes: Uther's concern is already canonized as a named warning thread. Advance it; do not present his fear as newly discovered.

scenes.title: The Tent
scenes.sceneFunction: Put the plague picture on Arthas's table, then let Jaina turn that picture into a narrow source plan before the room can collapse into fatalism.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Hearthglen proves the outbreak is accelerating and Stratholme is the next obvious city.
scenes.arcPressure: If Arthas hears only doom, he will march with only steel. If Jaina can still name one narrow path, it must be named now.

enrichment.detail: Uther should read as the broad, scarred first paladin still doing field-healer work with an older hammer at his side after Light's Vengeance passed to Arthas. Falric belongs in the tent as Arthas's King's Guard captain and loyal lieutenant, which makes the camp feel split between a mentor's moral authority and a prince's increasingly personal command chain.

beats.goal: Meet Arthas in person and get the first direct read on what he has become.
beats.conflict: Arthas is visibly changed before he says anything useful: damaged plate left unrepaired, eyes fixed too hard on the map, and the formal "Lady Proudmoore" landing on Jaina instead of her name. Sylvanas can see the history as a live wire without yet knowing its full shape.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas confirms Arthas is still an ally in form, but he is already moving like a commander whose answers come too fast.

beats.valueShift: warning in human form -> personal fault lines visible
newOnPageInformation: Arthas has stopped spending time on anything that does not move him toward the next decision.
The title "Lady Proudmoore" is a loaded choice, not neutral etiquette.
Uther stands behind the prince carrying concern in his grip rather than in his voice.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Arthas can still hear a plan that buys time rather than spends bodies.
The full shape of the Jaina-Arthas history gap.
beats.transitionIntent: briefing-start: once the room is set, the plague picture has to be laid down in the order the prince can act on.
beats.verifierNotes: The loaded 'Lady Proudmoore' address is callback material. Do not explain their full history on-page; let the title do the work.

beats.goal: Deliver the full Sylvanas-and-Jaina briefing to Arthas's command.
beats.conflict: The partnership still carries the crack, but the room does not have time for private damage. Sylvanas lays down the road, the cult trail, the witness chain, and Hearthglen's field read. Jaina adds the resonance mechanism, the ward mismatch, Kel'Thuzad's death, and the larger chain behind him.

outcome.summary: The full picture lands, but Arthas immediately treats it as movement fuel.

beats.valueShift: personal fault lines visible -> full danger on the table
newOnPageInformation: Arthas now has the full plague picture: Cult routes, resonance, ward failure, Lich King chain, and Kel'Thuzad's death.
Uther recognizes how cleanly Sylvanas and Jaina still work under pressure.
Arthas interrupts only for timing, roads, and where the next city will fail first.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the room contains any answer other than immediate movement.
Whether the theory can do more than explain why cities are dying.
beats.transitionIntent: narrow-path: the room needs either a brake or a smaller answer, and Jaina is the only person there who can try to name one.
beats.verifierNotes: The larger-war frame is shared knowledge from chapter14. Treat this briefing as field deployment of that knowledge, not a first reveal.

beats.goal: Put a practical plague answer on the table before Arthas commits the whole room to steel.
beats.conflict: Jaina does not ask for time to think. She asks for time to ride. The theory can still matter if they reach Stratholme's grain stores, bakeries, and main holding points before the city finishes seeding itself. It is not a full answer. It is a narrow one, ugly and late, but real.

outcome.summary: The room gets a practical plan, but it is a shrinking-window plan and Arthas refuses to wait on it.

beats.valueShift: full danger on the table -> one narrow path still open
newOnPageInformation: Jaina's countermeasure can be used against source nodes if she gets to them early enough.
The target is not the city in the abstract but specific grain stores, bakehouses, and flow points.
Jaina is trying to spend Kel'Thuzad's teaching against the plague at once instead of hoarding it.
Sylvanas sees that attempt and cannot fold it cleanly into her old sanctum read.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Stratholme is already too far seeded for the source plan to matter.
Whether Arthas hears head start as opportunity or as delay.
sensoryAnchors: Jaina's hand flattening on one quarter of the map
chalk on grain nodes rather than theory circles
Uther hearing one last door open
beats.transitionIntent: decision-pressure: once a narrow path exists, Arthas has to decide whether he waits for it or marches beside it.
beats.verifierNotes: Jaina's source-node answer is a narrow operational use of known mechanism, not a sudden complete cure.

beats.goal: Fix the relationship between Jaina's source plan and Arthas's army before the room breaks.
beats.conflict: Arthas refuses to bet Stratholme on waiting. The main column moves at once. Jaina and Cyndia can take the source-plan head start immediately, but when Sylvanas asks where he wants her, Arthas leaves the choice to her so long as the road stays clear. Uther, hearing exactly what that speed means, backs the smaller run and gets the outer lane opened.

outcome.summary: Jaina gets the attempt and Sylvanas gets a real choice, but the army is already moving and any answer will reach Stratholme under Arthas's shadow.

beats.valueShift: one narrow path still open -> double-track race
newOnPageInformation: Arthas's answer to uncertainty is still motion.
Uther materially backs the head-start attempt even while staying with the main column.
The source plan and the army march now run in parallel against the same clock.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Sylvanas will stay with the prince's march or spend her speed on Jaina's narrower attempt.
What Arthas will do if Jaina says too late at the city itself.
sensoryAnchors: staff officers moving before the sentence ends
Falric turning toward the tent flap on the first clear order
beats.transitionIntent: decision-under-motion: the answer has to be made in the departure rush before the column closes the window itself.
beats.verifierNotes: Advance the Arthas/Hearthglen race and Uther-warning pressure together. This is not the place to reset either thread into generic concern.
beats.verifierModification: Restored a real Sylvanas choice at the end of the tent scene and advanced Uther's warning thread with concrete support for the head-start lane.

beats.goal: Secure fresh horses, route clearance, and immediate departure ahead of the main column.
beats.conflict: The camp is already turning over too fast. Quartermasters are shouting, fresh tack is coming late, the outer lane has to be forced clear, and every minute spent here is a minute the city keeps eating its own bread.

outcome.summary: The head-start lane comes together, but the farewell pattern crosses from background oddity into something Sylvanas can no longer dismiss.

beats.valueShift: double-track race -> pattern back in play
newOnPageInformation: The camp can still throw a fast rider team out ahead of the army within minutes once the lane is ordered clear.
Cyndia hears the fourth farewell from an ordinary aide handling departure work.
The phrase now sits in Sylvanas's awareness as a pattern sharpened by urgency rather than a clue worth stopping for.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the pattern matters to the road they are about to take.
Whether Sylvanas will spend her own speed on Jaina's lane.
sensoryAnchors: fresh reins still stiff from the tack chest
route packet pinned under a knife
"May the stars keep your road" inside shouted orders
beats.transitionIntent: choice-launch: once the horses are under them, Sylvanas has to choose which clock she rides.
beats.verifierNotes: The farewell remains an unresolved repeating thread. Sharpen it as background sting, not foreground detour.
beats.verifierModification: Retuned the departure beat so the farewell callback rides under launch pressure instead of competing with it.

sceneLevelWarnings: Scene 2 continues the chapter's long scene-beat run. Keep the plague briefing, source-plan pivot, march decision, and departure rush sharply differentiated so the tent sequence does not flatten.

scenes.title: The Head Start
scenes.sceneFunction: Put Jaina's source plan on the road and let Sylvanas recognize, without any speech about it, that Jaina is trying to use the terrible knowledge the right way.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The main army is marching behind them and the city clock is already running.
scenes.arcPressure: Sylvanas still carries the sanctum misread, but the road now gives her new evidence she cannot ignore.

enrichment.detail: The Hearthglen-to-Stratholme push should feel like grain-country logistics ground rather than wilderness: roads built to carry carts, settlements connected by flour and market traffic, and every delay measured in loaves already moving outward. Thalassian field horses should read endurance-bred, responsive, and steady under magical strain, which makes the three-rider head start feel trained rather than ornamental.

beats.emotion: Hard urgency with no room left for the old silence to stay empty.
beats.dilemma: Stay with Arthas's obvious force, or ride with Jaina's narrower answer and test whether the terrible knowledge can still save anyone before the army arrives.
beats.decision: Ride with Jaina's attempt. Give the smaller plan every speed she can lend it.
beats.valueShift: pattern back in play -> the theory is being spent
newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas chooses the riskier smaller lane instead of the prince's obvious force.
Jaina is already cutting the city into actionable targets rather than admiring the theory behind them.
The partnership's first real repair stays entirely physical: who rides where when the clock starts.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the source plan can beat the city's spread.
Whether success or failure will change the sanctum read enough to matter.
sensoryAnchors: three horses stretching into a faster line
district names shouted over the wind
beats.transitionIntent: chapter-handoff: Chapter 16 opens on this same hard ride and the first attempt to break Stratholme's feed points before the city fully answers.
beats.verifierNotes: Do not overstate repair. The alignment is real because Sylvanas rides with the attempt, not because the trust scar has vanished.
beats.verifierModification: Reframed the close as an earned ride-with-her commitment and preserved chapter16's opener by pulling detailed saddle briefing out of this beat.

warnings: Beats 1-8 remain scene-heavy, so the draft must differentiate refugee cost, Hearthglen aftermath, healer warning, briefing friction, source-plan hope, and departure rush sharply.
Most scene beats still land as compromised progress; the prose needs distinct cost texture so the chapter does not flatten into one sustained yes-but line.
Beat 9 now closes on commitment and launch rather than detailed saddle planning, preserving chapter16's opener while keeping the handoff hot.

chapterBridge: Direct carry from chapter14's southbound ride: Jaina has already shared the larger war frame and the body-learning mechanism, both Silvermoon and Dalaran have been refused, the sanctum omission is still live inside the three-rider geometry, and that same hard road now breaks over Hearthglen before rolling straight toward Stratholme.

craftResearch.topic: battlefield aftermath into command briefing pacing
craftResearch.findings: Aftermath should stay in motion, briefing scenes need escalating decision pressure, and the launch should feel like a tempo jump rather than a reset.
craftResearch.source: My Story Doctor, Writer's Science, The Sketchbook Project

craftResearch.findings: Keep briefing scenes alive with conflicting aims, withheld uncertainty, body-language pressure, and a real decision point rather than static exposition.

craftResearch.topic: chapter transitions on a ticking clock
craftResearch.findings: A ticking-clock chapter should end on unresolved consequence or commitment and hand directly into the next scene with clear orientation.
craftResearch.source: Helping Writers Become Authors, Fiction University, Bookfox

craftResearch.findings: Long scene-heavy runs need at least one meaningful recalibration beat so the reader feels escalation rather than monotony.
A briefing scene should begin with uncertainty, escalate through disagreement or pressure, and end with a changed situation that forces immediate action.
craftResearch.source: Write It Great, suspense craft sources

pacing.notes: Opening pressure is strong and beat 6 supplies a real upward pivot, but beats 1-8 still form a long scene-heavy run that needs sharply differentiated cost texture in prose.

structure.notes: The chapter survives on strong macro-shape—arrival, warning, source-plan hope, launch—but needed repair to restore Sylvanas's real choice and to stop chapter15 from stealing chapter16's saddle-briefing opener.

character.notes: Sylvanas remains active and exteriorized correctly, Arthas reads as urgency made human, Jaina's field-use shift lands, and Uther now advances his warning through concrete support rather than repeated concern.

transitions.notes: All 9 beat-level transitionIntent chains verify. 4 intents were revised to sharpen arrival pressure, preserve the decision lane, subordinate the farewell callback, and preserve the chapter16 handoff.

continuity.notes: 0 new beats, 1 callback, 8 evolutions. The chapter correctly builds from established Eastweald/Hearthglen road-state, Arthas-Jaina history, Uther's warning, the macro-war frame, the mechanism answer, and the repeating farewell thread.

modifications.description: Compressed beat 1 so the chapter reaches Hearthglen pressure faster and does not re-introduce chapter14's road texture as fresh setup.
Reworked beats 7-9 so Sylvanas's final alignment with Jaina is a real late-chapter choice rather than a decision already made inside the tent.
Gave Uther a concrete contribution by backing the head-start lane, advancing his warning thread beyond generic visible worry.
Pulled detailed saddle-target planning out of beat 9 so chapter16 can still own the on-road operational briefing.
Annotated every beat with continuityStatus and targeted memory references, including explicit callback handling for Arthas/Jaina history and the repeating farewell thread.
